subject: Anthropology
title: Archaeology
description: A micro-lesson that makes Archaeology usable.

hook.text: A broken pot in the desert tells a story: who made it, what they ate, how they traded, and when they vanished.

buildup.text: Archaeology studies past human life through material remains — tools, buildings, trash, and landscapes.

discovery.text: It reconstructs entire civilizations from fragments. A seed reveals diet; a road reveals a trade empire.

twist.text: Archaeology gives voice to people who left no written records — the vast majority of human history.

climax.text: Without archaeology, 99% of human history would be silent. Objects speak when documents don't exist.

punchline.text: The past survives in what people left behind.

quiz.question: Why is archaeology important for understanding human history?
options: It recovers history from material remains when no texts exist
